results.final_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_orders
FROM orders
WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', order_date) = '2024' AND status = 'completed'

SELECT c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_revenue
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
WHERE o.status = 'completed'
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 5;

WITH category_revenue AS (
  SELECT p.category, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_revenue
  FROM orders o
  JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE o.status = 'completed'
  GROUP BY p.category
)
SELECT category, total_revenue, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_revenue DESC) AS revenue_rank
FROM category_revenue
ORDER BY revenue_rank ASC;

SELECT 
  p.category, 
  AVG(p.price) AS avg_price
FROM 
  products p
GROUP BY 
  p.category
HAVING 
  COUNT(p.product_id) > 2

SELECT customer_id, first_name 
FROM customers 
WHERE customer_id IN (
  SELECT customer_id 
  FROM orders 
  WHERE product_id IN (
    SELECT product_id 
    FROM products 
    WHERE category = 'Electronics'
  )
  INTERSECT
  SELECT customer_id 
  FROM orders 
  WHERE product_id IN (
    SELECT product_id 
    FROM products 
    WHERE category = 'Clothing'
  )
);

SELECT 
  STRFTIME('%Y-%m', order_date) AS month,
  SUM(total_amount) AS total_revenue,
  LAG(SUM(total_amount)) OVER (ORDER BY STRFTIME('%Y-%m', order_date)) AS prev_revenue,
  ROUND(COALESCE((SUM(total_amount) - LAG(SUM(total_amount)) OVER (ORDER BY STRFTIME('%Y-%m', order_date))) / 
                  NULLIF(LAG(SUM(total_amount)) OVER (ORDER BY STRFTIME('%Y-%m', order_date)), 0) * 100, NULL), 2) AS growth_pct
FROM orders
WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', order_date) = '2024' AND status = 'completed'
GROUP BY STRFTIME('%Y-%m', order_date)
ORDER BY STRFTIME('%Y-%m', order_date)

WITH ranked_orders AS (
  SELECT 
    c.city,
    p.product_name,
    SUM(o.quantity) AS total_quantity,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.city ORDER BY SUM(o.quantity) DESC, p.product_id ASC) AS rank
  FROM 
    customers c
  JOIN 
    orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
  JOIN 
    products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE 
    o.status = 'completed'
  GROUP BY 
    c.city, p.product_name, p.product_id
)
SELECT 
  city,
  product_name,
  total_quantity
FROM 
  ranked_orders
WHERE 
  rank = 1
ORDER BY 
  city ASC;

SELECT 
  c.customer_id, 
  c.first_name, 
  c.last_name, 
  SUM(CASE WHEN STRFTIME('%Y', o.order_date) = 2024 AND STRFTIME('%m', o.order_date) BETWEEN '01' AND '06' THEN o.total_amount ELSE 0 END) AS h1_revenue,
  SUM(CASE WHEN STRFTIME('%Y', o.order_date) = 2024 AND STRFTIME('%m', o.order_date) BETWEEN '07' AND '12' THEN o.total_amount ELSE 0 END) AS h2_revenue
FROM 
  customers c
  JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
WHERE 
  o.status = 'completed'
GROUP BY 
  c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
HAVING 
  h2_revenue > h1_revenue
ORDER BY 
  h2_revenue DESC
